calendar.matters:
Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustees To Render Accounting To Beneficiary And To Make Further Distribution To Beneficiary Pursuant To Court Order And Terms Of Trust And To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Jun-28-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Bonanno for Edmund Gapirio (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
John Mangini for Eileen Coffey and Diane Post (Trustees).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Mr. Gapirio's one third share of the $100,000.00 hold back/reserve should or should not be distributed to him and who (the two Trustees or all three individuals) would be responsible for any tax liability. Mr. Mangini explained that funds would be released three years after filing of the tax returns and that they were filed on different dates. Counsel also reported that an accounting has been provided, but not submitted to the Court for approval. The Court stated that Mr. Bonanno's Client has received a substantial distribution and can wait until the statute of limitations runs. The Court stays with the $100,000.00 reserve. The Petition to Make Further Distribution to Beneficiary, etc. is denied. If there are any problems with the accounting, the parties will present it to the Court.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustees To Render Accounting To Beneficiary And To Make Further Distribution To Beneficiary Pursuant To Court Order And Terms Of Trust And To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Continued To Jun-28-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Bonanno for Edmund Gapirio (Petitioner).
John Mangini for Eileen Coffey and Diane Post (Trustee).

Mr. Bonanno stated that he does not have a proposed order because he is trying to have an accountant review the accounting to see if there are any issues, that he should have a report within two to three weeks, that he will then know one way or another as to whether or not he will have any objections and that he will have a better idea once he gets the report. Mr. Mangini stated that $100,000.00 is being held back for a possible tax audit, that each beneficiary received $2,700,000.00 and that he will be providing Mr. Bonanno with information he requested within the next week or so. The Petition to Compel Successor Co-Trustees to Render Accounting to Beneficiary, etc. is continued to June 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the understanding that if the parties have not reached an analysis, the matter will be submitted or it will be determined if a further hearing is necessary.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustees To Render Accounting To Beneficiary And To Make Further Distribution To Beneficiary Pursuant To Court Order And Terms Of Trust And To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Continued To May-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustees To Render Accounting To Beneficiary And To Make Further Distribution To Beneficiary Pursuant To Court Order And Terms Of Trust And To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Continued To May-17-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustees To Render Accounting To Beneficiary And To Make Further Distribution To Beneficiary Pursuant To Court Order And Terms Of Trust And To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Apr-05-2017 Continued To May-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Jul-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonnano for (petitioner/beneficiary) Edmund Gapirio. John Mangini for (Trustees) Eileen Coffey & Diane Post.

Mr. Bonanno reported that they have narrowed their search for brokers and will be having a presentation, that certain assets have still not been distributed and that the trustees are to provide an accounting by the end of this month. The court stated that the issues left are not in the petition, that at this point we are just monitoring issues that are not before the court and that if other issues come up the parties can file an appropriate petition. Mr. Bonanno stated that the petition asks for distribution of assets and an accounting of what is left. Mr. Mangini reported that they have retained some assets in case work needs to be done on the property. The court stated that it will resolve the petition today by an order, that assets are to be distributed subject to a reasonable reserve by 30 days after the close of escrow and that an accounting is to be provided by 7/31/15. The petition to compel successor co-trustee to keep beneficiary reasonably informed of trust and its administration is taken under submission. Mr. Bonanno is to submit a proposed order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar May-19-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonanno for Edmund Gapirio. John Mangini for (Trustees) Diane Post & Eileen Coffey.

Mr. Mangini reported that they have received notice from the IRS, that there is no change in the taxes, that they have distributed $300,000.00 in securities, that the audit is complete, that the trust owns 90% of the property, that the parties have exchanged names of possible realtors and will interview and that they would like to keep a portion of the remaining cash in case any repairs are needed on the property and will distribute the balance. Mr. Bonanno reported that the realtor names exchanged were all from the same agency, confirmed that there has been distributions and that there is some issue regarding retrofitting on the Soft Story property but there has been no mandate from the building inspector as of this time. The Petition to Compel Successor Co-Trustee to Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Informed was continued to July 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that it will be taken off calendar prior to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Continued To May-19-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonanno for Edmund Gapirio. John Mangini for (trustees) Eileen Coffey & Diane Post.

Mr. Bonanno stated that they are requesting further distribution, that distribution is being held up by fear of taxation, explained his position as to why distribution would not jeopardize the trusts ability to pay taxes and that Mr. Gapirio should be allowed to enjoy his inheritance. Mr. Mangini reported that he filed an objection on Friday, that the IRS is conducting an exam of the 706, that issues have been resolved except for the valuation of property, that his accountant recommends they do not sell the property until the IRS finishes valuation and that substantial distribution has been made to date. Mr. Bonanno stated that the funds from the sale of the property would be more than ample to pay the taxes and that the IRS has a lien on the property to make sure. Mr. Mangini stated that he wants to hold onto the liquid assets, that if additional taxes are due he may want to pay it off with the liquid assets and requests that he be allowed to wait to see what the IRS says. The petition to compel successor co-trustee to keep beneficiary reasonably informed, etc., was continued to May 19, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court strongly suggested that Mr. Mangini figure out a way to distribute a significant amount of the liquid assets by that date or an order to so will be issued at that time.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Continued To Mar-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonanno for (petitioner & beneficiary) Edmund Gapirio. John Mangini for (trustees) Diane Post & Eileen Coffey.

Counsel reported that some issues have been resolved but many issues still remain to be resolved, that the IRS is auditing the estate tax return, that information requested has been provided to the IRS, that the IRS indicated that they will respond within 45 days, that the accountant recommends that sale of the property be postponed until the audit is complete, that they need to look for and agree upon a realtor, that all parties agree the property should be sold, there is an issue of timing, that when the property is sold an accounting will be done but that Mr. Mangini does not want to make distribution until all liabilities are known, etc. The petition to compel successor co-trustee to keep beneficiaries informed, etc. was continued to March 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to discuss the issues and for information to be provided to Mr. Bonanno with respect to copies of building inspector documents.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Continued To Jan-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonanno for Edmund Gapirio (Petitioner/Beneficiary). John Mangini for Diane Post and Eileen Coffee (Trustees).

Mr. Mangini stated that they are getting closer and closer, that the issue of a buyout is not going to happen so the property will be listed for sale, that they are working on logistics, that Mr. Bonanno wants to set up a conference with the accountant regarding the issue of taxes and that he will provide Mr. Bonanno copies of the taxes that have been filed. Counsel also spoke of their agreement regarding additional distribution and the costs for retro fitting/upgrades to the property. The Parties will file a stipulation and submit a proposed order. The Petition to Compel Successor Co-Trustee to Keep Beneficiary Informed was continued to January 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on negotiations and setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Continued To Nov-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonanno for (petitioner & beneficiary) Edmund Gapirio. John Mangini for (trustees) Eileen Coffey & Diane Post.

Mr. Bonanno reported that they are closer to a resolution, are negotiating what to do with the real property and the disposition of the remaining assets. Mr. Mangini reported that the estate tax return was filed in March, they are waiting for the closing letter from the IRS, they are holding cash in case of tax issues and that the trustees would like to keep the and are waiting for the appraisal for a possible buyout of the beneficiaries. The petition to compel successor co-trustee to keep beneficiary reasonably informed was continued to November 18, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jack Bonnano for Edmund Gapirio (Petitioner/Beneficiary). John Mangini for Eileen Coffey and Diane Post, (Trustees).

Mr. Bonnano stated that they have made a great deal of progress and are in the process of resolving the issue of the Laguna Street property. Both counsel requested a sixty day continuance. The petition to compel successor co-trustee to keep beneficiary reasonably informed, etc. was continued to October 1, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Successor Co-Trustee To Keep Beneficiary Reasonably Infromed Of Trust And Its Administration; Etc.
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Continued To Jul-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Apperances: Jack Bonanno for Petitioner Edmund Gapirio. John Mangini for Trustees Eileen Coffey and Diane Post.

The Court questioned why the venue of the case is San Francisco. Mr. Bonanno reported that the major assets which is a 12 unit rental is located in San Francisco and that they day to day activities occur in San Francisco. Furthermore, Mr. Bonanno reported that progress in the form of distributions have been made and that the full accounting issue has been resolved. Mr. Mangini reported that the issue of venue is a battle that he does not want to get involved with and that his main concern is his clients deciding if they want to buy out the third beneficiaries interest in the apartment building or selling the apartment building.

Mr. Bonanno addressed the issue of Mr. Melvin Giorgi and stated that he has passed away and that the requirement of all beneficiaries being noticed have been met. The Court advised that is this case moves forward, then this information may need to be filed. The Court continued the petition to compel successor co-trustee to keep beneficiary reasonably informed of trust and its administration to July 23, 2014, in Dept. 204 at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
